full_suite.failure_notes: Six TCP loopback and two AF_UNIX tests fail because the managed sandbox denies socket bind. All eight fail identically in a git-archive snapshot of HEAD 58b10fdb651a6c40481c6b0de51e263ba1f31ad5; proof is in runs/lanes/ballarc_scale_guard_20260715/head_socket_failures.stdout.

honest_issues: VERIFIED=0 remains binding; this is bounded abstention, not a promotion or accuracy result.
The full-game result is heavily degraded: 187/188 segments are missing outcomes, world coverage is 50 frames, and render sample count is 0.
The production 5s default can abstain on otherwise valid fits exceeding 5s; Wolverine parity used a deliberate non-tripping 30s budget.
The deadline is cooperative, not an OS-level preemptive kill; observed worst overrun was 0.0591s.
The optional ball_physics3d diagnostic is explicitly missing on degraded runs to preserve the end-to-end bound.
The wide suite is not literally green, although every failure was proven identical at HEAD.
Storage audit remains red for 76 absent allowlisted historical files, with zero unknown large files.
No branch, commit, promotion, GT/training use, or out-of-fence code edit occurred.
next: Retain the typed safety guard. Evaluate vectorized/adaptive prediction and total-pool prefiltering separately under frozen accuracy and parity gates if arc recovery—not bounded degradation—is required.
